126312.explanation: It is important that everyone feels represented in the municipal government. Of course, the aldermen and mayor must be well qualified, regardless of their gender.

126313.explanation: We find it very important that residents can participate in decisions about how things are done here in Waalre. When an issue is highly significant, a referendum can be a good and fair way to make a decision. We also want to promote other forms of citizen participation.

126316.explanation: We encourage our farmers to switch to nature-inclusive or organic farming. By allowing additional income such as camping, small-scale hospitality, or the sale of local products, we help facilitate this transition.

126317.explanation: A sustainable future is important to all of us. The municipality must contribute to this, especially for residents with lower incomes.

126320.explanation: It is important that farmers contribute to proper housing for the people who work for them. This can also be outside their own property. There is no place in our municipality for exploitation and nuisance.

126322.explanation: Many people experience nuisance from wood burning in our municipality. It is also harmful to the environment. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to reduce wood burning so that the air is healthy to breathe for everyone. The wood burning guide is leading in this.

126324.explanation: The municipality should investigate how New Year's Eve without fireworks can be made attractive for everyone.

126325.explanation: If bulky waste is collected, we have more control over its reuse. Additionally, less is dumped in nature. A win-win for the environment.

126328.explanation: There is a huge housing shortage and an extra home on the property can be a good solution. As an additional income source or as a suitable place for a parent who can no longer live independently. We want to make this possible for farmers who want it.

126335.explanation: Living and moving around in Waalre should be pleasant for everyone. To guarantee that safety, for example in traffic, enforcement is necessary.

126336.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But more cameras are not the right solution in our opinion. Your privacy is a great asset that should not be violated lightly. Therefore, we prefer more street enforcement officers who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

126338.explanation: Everyone should be able to participate in our municipality. Having a nice home is part of that. Starters can use a helping hand.

126341.explanation: Using holiday homes is a good temporary solution to alleviate the housing shortage. However, it is important to ensure enough new homes are built so that everyone can live in a real house.

126342.explanation: Besides the expansion locations Waalre North and Ekenrooi South, new homes should only be built in the current centers and neighborhoods. We do this to protect nature and the landscape.

126343.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don't want to work. They need a stable foundation and trust from the municipality. By imposing fewer rules on them, they can focus more on their future and get back on track.

126347.explanation: Switching to electric driving should become more attractive. For this, it is important that there are enough charging stations.

126348.explanation: Lowering the speed limit is better for everyone. It is safer for playing children, pedestrians, cyclists, and drivers. It is also better for air quality, health, and the environment. In addition, having the same speed limit everywhere in our municipality is clearer.

126349.explanation: We find it very important that everyone can receive good care in our municipality. Customization is our approach.

126350.explanation: In providing help, the quality is especially important. Good care means lower costs in the long term.

126353.explanation: We align the tourist tax with that of Eindhoven. This way we have more money to invest in our village without having to increase taxes for residents.

126356.explanation: People with the greatest need should get a home first, refugees included.

126361.explanation: The Netherlands is a water country and every child should learn to swim. Including children whose parents cannot afford swimming lessons. Unfortunately, this is still not fairly arranged. The best solution: school swimming for every child, with support from the municipality.

126362.explanation: Dialect is a valuable form of culture. Schools should be free to decide whether to pay attention to the Brabant dialect.

126366.explanation: The municipality must supervise room rentals. The homes must be of good quality for the residents and must not cause nuisance to the surroundings.

126369.explanation: There must be a good place for the valuable collection of the Waalre Museum. Art and history are important and must be protected.

126371.explanation: The municipality must invest in greening our municipality. Whenever possible, native species should be used to strengthen biodiversity.

126312.explanation: Quality is more important than being male or female.

126313.explanation: Citizen participation is very important to us. Holding a referendum can be an option but is certainly not mandatory.

126316.explanation: Farmers should have broad opportunities to operate a secondary activity alongside their farm. When the farm is partly or entirely ceased, there should be the possibility to establish a suitable new business or activity.

126317.explanation: The municipality might be able to facilitate in another way.

126320.explanation: Apart from that, in Waalre we do not have agricultural or horticultural businesses employing seasonal workers.

126322.explanation: But with observance of the wood burning guide.

126324.explanation: In principle, I agree, but there is a cost attached to that.

126325.explanation: The municipality can facilitate bulky waste collection, but it should not be collected for free. Here too, the polluter pays.

126328.explanation: We are against further paving over of the rural area.

126335.explanation: The number of enforcement officers (boa's) in Waalre seems sufficient to us. On the other hand, their visibility should be increased.

126336.explanation: Waalre does not use camera surveillance. So far, there has been no need demonstrated for it.

126338.explanation: Waalre must first build sufficient social rental and affordable owner-occupied homes. When those homes become available, a starter loan can be considered.

126341.explanation: Despite the tight housing market, this seems a bad development to us.

126342.explanation: Where we assume that with the existing village boundaries, the 'built-up area' is meant.

126343.explanation: Not everyone receiving benefits can do social work. This can be stimulated on a voluntary basis.

126347.explanation: Work on this is already underway.

126348.explanation: Almost achieved for the entire built-up area, but drivers do not adhere to it. Moreover, enforcement is not possible.

126349.explanation: It is already difficult enough to get enough caregivers active. This choice is not feasible and takes us too far.

126350.explanation: Besides quality, price is important. The costs of youth care are skyrocketing.

126353.explanation: The tourist tax can be increased because residents are not directly affected, and with the proceeds, facilities can be maintained and/or improved.

126356.explanation: Disagree because this leads to unequal situations. Residents who have been waiting a long time for social housing are disadvantaged. For us, it is: equal treatment for all.

126361.explanation: Reintroducing school swimming would be a good thing. In principle, this is a national responsibility and the government should arrange this and allocate funds for it.

126362.explanation: The Brabant dialect does not exist. So, paying attention to the local dialect in every Brabant town or village would be an impossible task. Schools should first focus more on reading, writing, and understanding the Dutch language.

126366.explanation: Renting out individual rooms can lead to undesirable situations. We prefer to work with permits so that the rental can be regulated.

126369.explanation: We did not cooperate in ending the subsidy to the Waalre Museum. As a result, the museum must close as of January 1, 2026. The museum holds the collections on loan and they will return to the rightful owners.

126371.explanation: Green maintenance can always improve. However, investing even more money in green maintenance means cutting back on other tasks. Better to look at how public spaces can be designed to be easier to maintain.

126312.explanation: It is not about the ratio of men to women in the board but about the qualities of the board members.

126313.explanation: Referendums reduce complex decisions to a yes or no choice, which does not do justice to the complexity of many proposals. In elections, people can choose representatives they trust to make decisions about the many possible proposals.

126316.explanation: Provided it does not hinder the operation of their farms and they also comply with laws and regulations for retail, recreation, and the tourism sector.

126317.explanation: No financial support but assistance with collaboration, permits, and implementation.

126320.explanation: They must provide decent housing, but it does not have to be on their own property. It is important that when the employment ends, the worker is not immediately left homeless.

126322.explanation: No ban, but burning must comply with the burn guide.

126324.explanation: If there is a need for it in the village, this is a good way to promote community spirit.

126325.explanation: It must remain possible to bring bulky waste to the recycling center, and it must be quick and easy to find the costs for bringing waste to the recycling center.

126328.explanation: Each case must be examined individually. Possible restrictions from and for neighboring farms must also be considered.

126335.explanation: If there are concrete signals that residents feel unsafe, increasing the number of enforcement officers can enhance residents' sense of security. They can also ensure a faster response to nuisance, although they need extra resources and protection to do their work safely.

126336.explanation: There are no acute problems in Waalre that require this now. More camera surveillance is only needed for a clear, acute problem, and only as a last resort if other measures like extra lighting do not work. It must not unnecessarily infringe on people’s privacy.

126338.explanation: It is more important first to ensure sufficient housing supply for starters than to provide a starter loan. If it turns out that despite sufficient supply, starters lack financial means, a starter loan can be a solution.

126341.explanation: This is not the purpose of a holiday park.

126342.explanation: To build enough houses, we must build beyond the existing borders. We must, however, respect the green character of Waalre and not build it all over.

126343.explanation: People on social assistance must do everything possible to leave it. If this is possible; for example, a single mother with young children should be subject to different requirements. It remains a matter of individual assessment.

126347.explanation: It must be easy to use a parking space as a charging spot for an electric car.

126348.explanation: On most roads, the speed must be reduced to 30 km/h. The design of the roads must match this. These roads must also be safe for cycling.

126349.explanation: Healthcare must be affordable and accessible. Where possible, consideration is given to religion, culture, and beliefs.

126350.explanation: Quality and continuity are more important than price.

126353.explanation: The tourist tax was recently increased and should not be raised further given the VAT increase.

126356.explanation: We follow the national guidelines here.

126361.explanation: We want everyone to be able to swim, so everyone should be able to take swimming lessons. If necessary, with a contribution from the municipality.

126362.explanation: Attention should be given to Brabant’s cultural heritage, including the Brabant dialect. Of course, this is not more important than attention to math and language.

126366.explanation: It must be possible to rent out rooms in the house where you live yourself. No student housing should be realized in Waalre. Smaller independent housing units should be developed for starters, etc.

126369.explanation: The collection must remain available to our residents but does not necessarily have to be always visible.

126371.explanation: More money is not needed. Careful planning of maintenance is important to limit inconvenience during the autumn months.

126312.explanation: A diverse board makes better decisions. We want a municipality where everyone’s talents count and everyone feels at home.

126313.explanation: We prefer citizen councils and early participation. This way, residents can really have a say and contribute ideas before decisions are finalized, instead of only voting yes or no afterward.

126316.explanation: We want to give our farmers more space to do things with their land, especially if it contributes to nature-inclusive farming and green recreation.

126317.explanation: Producing energy together is the future. We support groups of residents who do this, ensuring that the benefits remain local.

126320.explanation: A good employer also ensures decent, humane housing. If this can be on their own property, it does not reduce housing availability in Waalre.

126322.explanation: We focus on awareness through burning alerts and switching to cleaner heat sources. A ban goes too far for now, but air quality and residents’ health are priorities.

126324.explanation: A central, professional fireworks or light show is a safe and festive alternative to consumer fireworks. This way we keep the tradition of celebrating together but with less nuisance and damage.

126325.explanation: It is fair if you pay for your own waste. That way, people separate waste better and throw away less. We do ensure the recycling center remains accessible and that waste can be collected at home for a fee (via Big Bags).

126328.explanation: We are open to splitting farms or small-scale construction (e.g., courtyard clusters). The landscape must improve as a result. We want to prevent much construction in the rural area. Quality comes first.

126335.explanation: A feeling of safety in the neighborhood is important. Sufficient neighborhood police officers and enforcement officers who are visible and approachable to residents. That way, Waalre remains safe with the people we already have.

126336.explanation: In a village like Waalre, camera surveillance is not currently necessary. We choose privacy and freedom. We solve safety by looking out for each other well, not by installing cameras everywhere.

126338.explanation: A starter loan is an option, but we choose a different path. We want to build more affordable (rental) homes. With more housing, we help young people secure their own place in Waalre.

126341.explanation: We do not want permanent residence in recreational parks in principle. Because we want the quality of recreation and nature to remain good. In emergencies, we seek temporary solutions.

126342.explanation: We are careful with the rural area and prefer to build within the village. Infill development is preferred over expansion. But this is insufficient. The larger housing plans needed to solve the housing shortage border the village limits.

126343.explanation: We believe in opportunities, not obligations. We prefer to help people into paid work that suits them through education and guidance. Volunteer work is appreciated and encouraged but must remain a free choice. Otherwise, it cannot be called 'voluntary'.

126347.explanation: Electric driving is the future. We ensure a comprehensive network of charging stations in public spaces. Then residents and visitors can easily charge their cars and air quality improves.

126348.explanation: Safety comes first. We want 30 km/h to become the norm in residential areas. Then it becomes safer for cyclists, pedestrians, and playing children. There is also less noise nuisance and cleaner air.

126349.explanation: Care must primarily focus on health and well-being, regardless of religion, culture, or belief.

126350.explanation: Quality of care always comes first. For vulnerable children, price must not be the most important factor. We invest in good youth care to prevent worse problems.

126353.explanation: Tourists use our facilities and beautiful surroundings. It is fair that they contribute to their maintenance through a tourist tax.

126356.explanation: Status holders have the right to housing, just like other housing seekers. We do not want to pit groups against each other but solve the housing shortage by building faster and more (temporary) homes for everyone.

126361.explanation: We believe all children should be able to obtain their swimming diploma. With support like the Youth Fund for Sport & Culture and participation contributions if parents cannot pay.

126362.explanation: Language and traditions are part of who we are. If schools want to pay attention to the dialect themselves, we think that is a fine idea. It enriches education and creates connection with the region. But it is not the municipality’s responsibility to fund this education.

126366.explanation: We want to prevent nuisance in residential areas and safeguard livability. 'Subdivision into multiple rooms' by professional landlords can lead to issues like parking pressure and noise nuisance.

126369.explanation: The story of Waalre must remain visible. That can be done in many ways: through small exhibitions in the village, digitization, or cooperation with museums in the region. We find it very important that Waalre’s heritage remains in the region so it does not lose its value.

126371.explanation: A green municipality requires good maintenance. We not only want to plant more greenery but also ensure it is well cared for. This increases living enjoyment and appreciation of our beautiful outdoor spaces.

126312.explanation: For an independent municipality Waalre, the quality of its leaders must come first.

126313.explanation: We want to involve residents more in important decision-making so that all opinions are heard, but the final decision remains with the elected representatives in the municipal council.

126316.explanation: We want fewer restrictive rules for entrepreneurs. Therefore, we are positive towards such initiatives, which also contribute to tourism and recreation and thus to the transition of the rural area.

126317.explanation: We do not consider this a task for the municipality. For cooperative energy generation, for example, a call can be made on the national SCE subsidy scheme.

126320.explanation: We have good (basic) housing rules in the Netherlands. In Waalre, it concerns only small numbers at most; we see no reason for deviating policy.

126322.explanation: VVD Aalst-Waalre is against a ban on wood burning.

126324.explanation: For a small municipality like Waalre, a fireworks show that is attractive enough to draw people is too expensive.

126325.explanation: We want residents to be able to dispose of more types of waste within the municipality of Waalre. But free bulky waste collection causes an increase in waste charges and thus higher costs, also for people who do not use this service.

126328.explanation: We want to provide space for agricultural entrepreneurs to build homes on their own land when their business ends. This must be done within clear rules. No uncontrolled growth, but tailor-made solutions: quality over quantity.

126335.explanation: VVD Aalst-Waalre wants more enforcement officers on the street and in the rural area who tackle nuisance and enforce rules. This contributes to a feeling of safety.

126336.explanation: VVD Aalst-Waalre wants to be able to deploy cameras targeted at locations where nuisance or vandalism regularly occur. Camera surveillance increases the chance of detection and offers monitoring at risk locations.

126338.explanation: Especially in these times, when starters and young families find it very difficult to find affordable homeownership, it is important that the municipality can offer a helping hand.

126341.explanation: Allowing residential use of holiday homes can help alleviate the housing shortage. The supply in Waalre is small and manageable and thus residence, via a temporary exemption, is enforceable.

126342.explanation: There is great demand for affordable housing in Aalst-Waalre and the surrounding Brainport region. We therefore want to seize all housing opportunities and not exclude developments in advance.

126343.explanation: Social assistance is a safety net for those who really need it. It is important that our society offers that net, but we also ask for a counter-performance. This way, you contribute to society, meet new people, and maintain routine in your life.

126347.explanation: We want to promote greening of the vehicle fleet by supporting electric driving and ensuring a wide supply of public charging points. Everyone should be able to charge their electric car within walking distance of their home.

126348.explanation: Where a maximum speed of 30 km/h applies, it must be effectively enforced. Because many roads need to be redesigned for this, with associated costs, we want to continue allowing 50 km/h on main roads where that is safe. This also promotes traffic flow and accessibility.

126349.explanation: We believe that providing suitable care is not the municipality’s responsibility but that of health insurers.

126350.explanation: Efficient use of municipal resources also means looking at quality. So, we do not want to select solely on price. Cheap can otherwise become expensive.

126353.explanation: The tourist tax in Waalre has already been significantly increased in recent years. We currently see no reason to raise it further.

126356.explanation: Social rental homes should be available first for residents of Aalst-Waalre or people with vital social professions, such as police officers, nurses, and teachers. We want to abolish the priority scheme for migrants with a residence permit.

126361.explanation: We see the importance of school swimming, but the absence of a public swimming pool in Aalst-Waalre is too great an obstacle to organize this properly.

126362.explanation: The municipality does not interfere with the content of the education program. Primary schools that wish to do so can pay attention to the Brabant dialect themselves, but we believe the municipality should not facilitate this.

126366.explanation: We believe that in this time of severe housing shortage, room rentals can be a means to make extra living space available. A ban may be useful only when room rentals lead to nuisance, but in our opinion, this is not the case in Waalre.

126369.explanation: We regret that the Waalre Museum has closed, but we conclude from this that there was apparently insufficient interest in the collection. Therefore, we do not consider it desirable for the municipality to invest in it after all.

126371.explanation: We see more value in good cooperation with residents than simply spending more money. Initiatives from residents and organizations to green and maintain streets or neighborhoods are wholeheartedly supported.

126312.explanation: The quality of the aldermen matters: preferably a local alderman who deals respectfully with dissent. Both behind the scenes and in public. An alderman who also listens to people without a loud voice. An alderman who dares to act independently of civil servants.

126313.explanation: It strongly depends on the subject whether it is useful and it can only be applied to a limited extent. For all important subjects, residents must receive information about various aspects. Public meetings, where listening occurs, are therefore at least as important as a referendum.

126316.explanation: It is undesirable for a campsite to be established everywhere. That would affect the landscape and possibly the living enjoyment of others too much. A small shop with local farmer products could be allowed everywhere. The municipality can decide to use as many local products as possible itself.

126317.explanation: There is already a subsidy for sustainable energy such as the VAT refund. Through cooperation, costs become lower and yields higher, so financial support is less relevant. However, the municipality can encourage local sharing of knowledge about jointly generating and using energy.

126320.explanation: This situation no longer occurs in Waalre. But when it did occur during the asparagus picking, it was neatly arranged in this manner.

126322.explanation: But burning wet wood or burning during fog causes a lot of nuisance. That is undesirable. Share knowledge with residents about desired burning behavior. Use information from the Burning Guide of the National Institute for Public Health and the Environment.

126324.explanation: If many residents express the desire for this, even if the costs are known, the municipal executive can be asked via a motion to organize a central fireworks show, as was done in Voorschoten. To date, no such wishes have been expressed by residents.

126325.explanation: The fee for dumping bulky waste in Veldhoven is limited and collection within Waalre is also possible at limited costs. There are no known problems with illegal dumping of bulky waste.

126328.explanation: Unbridled urbanization of the countryside is undesirable. Every field can thus become an estate that is subsequently inaccessible and invisible to village residents. (Pre-)mantelzorg houses must be possible in rural areas just as they are within built-up areas.

126335.explanation: For now, there is no need to increase the number of enforcement officers. It would be nice if they would use bicycles more instead of cars. On a bike, they are easier to recognize and can be approached. It's great that the local newspaper De Schakel also gives publicity to the enforcement officers.

126336.explanation: ZW14 sees little added value in more camera surveillance. Residents can also contribute themselves with, for example, video doorbells, security cameras, and active neighborhood apps.

126338.explanation: A starter loan can be useful for new-build homes. Practical experience shows that for existing houses it is difficult and brings many extra costs. More important is to include starter homes in new construction plans and ensure these remain available for starters and local residents.

126341.explanation: There are few holiday homes in Waalre, and they should remain holiday homes. Under conditions, they may be used as a residential address.

126342.explanation: Preserve the green character of Waalre by not building everything up. Instead, look at alternatives such as more small residential towers that fit Waalre, and possibly limited high-rise buildings along highways. Also, make better use of existing buildings, by splitting homes, partial rental, etc.

126343.explanation: Of course, this only concerns people with social assistance benefits who are capable of this. If social work does not immediately suit someone or he/she is not immediately suitable, the municipality can organize a learning path for them.

126347.explanation: It is fine if more charging points for electric cars are created, but non-electric cars should also be able to park there. ZW14 wants to encourage the installation of cable ducts and private charging stations at residents’ homes, so that excess generated energy can also be used by others.

126348.explanation: Preferably everyone within built-up areas drives a maximum of 30 km/h, but that is not realistic. However, on road surfaces where 30 km/h applies – especially around schools – strict enforcement is required. Eventually, all 30 km/h roads should have designs that enforce slow driving.

126349.explanation: Care workers are trained to work with many different people. It is undesirable if segregation arises here. ZW14 also wants to reduce the number of care providers so that better control over quality/effectiveness and price is possible.

126350.explanation: The municipality must of course critically review the price, but effectiveness/quality is most important. Organizations with high overhead or inappropriate profits must be excluded. The number of youth care organizations should also be limited to enable better control.

126353.explanation: Waalre aligns the tourist tax with that of Eindhoven. Currently, small accommodations, such as Bed & Breakfasts, do not pay per overnight guest, whereas in Eindhoven they do. This should be aligned too, which will slightly increase revenues.

126356.explanation: The municipality must legally allocate a number of refugees with residence permits (‘status holders’) a home. This is a small portion of the social rental homes that become available. Proper allocation is important given the housing shortage: no family homes for single status holders!

126361.explanation: Obtaining a swimming diploma costs a lot of money and can be difficult for parents to arrange. Unfortunately, more and more children in our water country have no swimming diploma as a result. It would be good to see if schools can play a role in solving this.

126362.explanation: The Brabant dialect is part of our culture. Attention to culture including the Brabant dialect is valuable to us. 'Niks mis mi' (Nothing wrong with me).

126366.explanation: ZW14 actually wants more living spaces to become available in this way for habitation, provided in manageable numbers. At a certain point in someone's housing career, this can fit well. However, it must be prevented that whole streets become 'roomed out' and maximum social rent prices apply.

126369.explanation: Unfortunately, there is insufficient interest in the collection of the Waalre Museum, making the museum not viable.

126371.explanation: Waalre has a maintenance plan for greenery including sufficient financial resources. During reconstruction of roads and neighborhoods, more greenery is created to counter heat stress and retain rainwater longer. So, more attention (and thus more money) is already available for greenery.

statements.title: The Waalre Museum's collection should always remain on display for residents.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: In Waalre the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain allowed.

titleParts.text: In Waalre moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Waalre should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Residents of Waalre should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Waalre moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
There should be more camera surveillance in Waalre.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality must make it possible for all primary schools to include attention to the Brabant dialect.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
Waalre should spend more money on maintaining green spaces in the municipality.
